name: Solterra: Guildblade
tagline: A solo combat-RPG engine — climb the Adventurers' Charter, one quest at a time.
author: Solterra / Guildblade (player-supplied)
summary: Take guild quests, battle through monsters, climb the ranks Tin→Star-forged, and watch a Unique Power evolve into legend. Action-first, gambit-driven combat run by a single GM voice (Skyra). Play opens at the Adventurers' Charter chapterhouse in the port city of Kalthas.

gm.notes: GM-ONLY. Full monster stat blocks + scaling/variant tables. The loader should make this available to the NARRATOR as reference context (retrieval or pinned), but it must NEVER be written into player-facing world_state or shown in the UI. The player's own discovered-creature notes are a separate, initially-empty section: seed.bestiary below. See templates/README.md → 'The bestiary gap'.

characterSchema._doc: Reference shape only — NOT seeded. The §2 Character Forge fills these at session start (seed.character stays {}). Listed so the future loader and CharacterTab know the keys this template's narrator will produce.

seed._doc: Each key maps to a world_state.json section that fills a UI tab. The loader should merge these into a fresh world_state (see templates/README.md → 'Seed → world_state mapping'). 'bestiary' is a NEW section that does not yet exist in the schema.

npcs.name: Skyra
npcs.notes: The quiet ledger-keeper and rank-announcer — Solterra's interface made diegetic. Speaks rarely (rank-ups, Awakenings, the Thread, the welcome): two lines at most. Grants the once-per-rank mercy when the player falls (Skyra's Thread).

npcs.name: The Charter Clerk
npcs.notes: Pays out quest rewards, stocks rank-appropriate gear and consumables, and keeps the board chalked. The face of the Adventurers' Charter in Kalthas.

locations.name: Kalthas
locations.description: A vast sunlit port where all three worlds share the docks — Coalition, Deep Wood Folk, and the Sundered Flag. The starting city; the Charter's chapterhouse here never closes.

locations.name: Charter Chapterhouse
locations.description: The Adventurers' Charter's house in Kalthas. Holds the quest board (3 postings at a time), a clerk who pays out, a notice wall of free rumor-hooks, and beds for plated members.

lorebook.title: Solterra — The World in Brief
lorebook.content: Solterra, the Age of Legends. The Coalition holds the cities and roads; the Deep Wood Folk keep the wilds and old ways; the Sundered Flag sails free and answers to no one. Between pockets of civilization, aether-storms breed monsters in the wild places — and culling them is the trade of the Adventurers' Charter. Factions are texture, not bookkeeping: no reputation scores, just people who remember how you treated them.

lorebook.title: The Adventurers' Charter
lorebook.content: The guild whose plate the player carries. Ranks by plate: Tin E · Copper D · Silver C · Gold B · Mithril A · Star-forged S. The chapterhouse offers the board (exactly 3 quests per visit), a clerk who pays out, a notice wall of rumors (free hooks), and beds for plated members. A member may take quests up to their rank +1; punching up pays +50% quest XP. Quest types: CULL · HUNT · DELVE · ESCORT · RECOVER · STRANGE.

lorebook.title: Currency of Solterra
lorebook.content: 1 Gold Crown (GC) = 20 Silver Sails (SS) = 200 Copper Bits (CB). Quest pay and gear prices are quoted in these.

lorebook.title: Kalthas — The Port City
lorebook.content: Where play opens: a vast sunlit port where Coalition, Deep Wood Folk, and Sundered Flag all share the docks. The Charter's chapterhouse here never closes. The hub the player returns to between quests for rest, pay, and the next board.

lorebook.title: Skyra — The System's Voice
lorebook.content: The quiet voice of the system: ledger-keeper, rank-announcer, keeper of the once-per-rank mercy. She is seasoning, not narration — two lines at most, and rarely. She speaks at rank-ups, Awakenings, the welcome, and when she pulls the Thread (the once-per-rank-tier save from death).

lorebook.title: The Gambit Rule — Heart of Combat
lorebook.content: When the player uses a skill, item, power, or the arena's Canvas in a way that is creative, fictionally plausible, and specific, the GM grants one of: Advantage, +1d6 effect, ignore Guard, or a rider (trip, blind, burn, disarm). An exceptional gambit may simply succeed. Every honored gambit: +5 XP. The attempt is never punished — if implausible, the GM says in one line what would make it work. Monsters carry Traits that invite counters; bosses carry a hidden Flaw that rewards observation.

lorebook.title: The Unique Power & Awakening
lorebook.content: Each character has one Unique Power, graded to Rank E at creation: a name, a reliable application, a stretch application, and a STA/AET cost. It out-scales everything they own. At each rank-up the power Awakens — 2–3 named evolution paths shaped by how the player actually used it. At C it takes a Second Form (renamed, transformed); at A, its Apex.

session_beats: Session start: the player comes to the Adventurers' Charter chapterhouse in the port city of Kalthas to take up a tin plate. Character creation (the Forge) runs first; then the tin plate is pressed into their palm and the board is chalked with three quests.

onboarding.openingHint: The player has just finished the Character Forge in the app. Give Skyra's welcome — two lines, no more — and confirm their character in-fiction. Render a STATUS block from the exact numbers on the sheet. Then press the tin (Rank E) plate into their palm at the Adventurers' Charter chapterhouse in the port city of Kalthas, and chalk the board with exactly three rank-appropriate quests (mix of CULL / HUNT / DELVE / ESCORT / RECOVER / STRANGE). Invite them to pick one or explore the chapterhouse. Do NOT re-run any part of character creation.
onboarding.firstMessageHint: This is the unprompted OPENING message (§12 step 1). Do exactly two things, then STOP and wait for the player. (1) Skyra's welcome — two lines, no more. (2) Present the full Character Forge from §2 as CHOICES THE PLAYER MUST MAKE: ask for Identity (name, pronouns, look, one line of past); list the Race options; list the Origin options; ask them to allocate 50 stat points across the six stats (min 3, max 12); list the skills and have them choose 3; ask them to describe a Unique Power concept. HARD RULES for this message: do NOT invent, assume, or pre-fill the player's character — no chosen name, race, origin, or stats; do NOT roll or pre-allocate stats; do NOT render a completed character sheet or a STATUS block; do NOT use placeholder brackets like [Name]/[Race] as if the sheet were done. Do NOT mention the Kalthas chapterhouse, hand over the tin plate, or post the quest board — those belong ONLY to the NEXT message, after the player has answered (§12 step 2). End by inviting the player to reply with their choices.
